@@ -652,6 +652,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-e76uy","title":"Fail closed on btrfs extent free ownership/accounting errors","description":"BtrfsExtentAllocator::free_extent deletes extent-tree items before proving the extent belongs to a registered block group, falls back to delayed-ref root 0 when no owner exists, and uses saturating_sub for used_bytes. Validate ownership/accounting before mutation and add regressions that invalid frees leave tree state, accounting, and delayed refs unchanged.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T07:14:28.297526388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T07:20:07.725090369Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T07:20:07.722986283Z","close_reason":"Validated btrfs extent free ownership/accounting before mutation, removed delayed-ref root 0 fallback for no-owner frees, replaced saturating used_bytes free accounting with checked underflow rejection, and added regressions proving invalid frees leave tree state/accounting/delayed refs unchanged. Validation passed: focused ffs-btrfs regressions, fmt, diff-check, UBS touched file, workspace check, and workspace clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-e7dak","title":"btrfs INO_LOOKUP treeid parity","description":"Bring BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP closer to Linux behavior: treeid=0 must resolve to the mounted subvolume tree id, root-object lookups must return that resolved tree id, and explicit treeid lookups should walk the requested btrfs fs tree instead of rejecting cross-subvolume requests when the root tree contains the target ROOT_ITEM. Update focused core coverage and FEATURE_PARITY evidence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T00:54:09.367156995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:08:39.507889009Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:08:39.506015892Z","close_reason":"Implemented BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP treeid parity with focused core tests and spec/parity docs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","parity","porting"]}
 {"id":"bd-e8kp1","title":"add mounted-path ext4 readlink-on-non-symlink EINVAL coverage","description":"Survey finding: ext4 mounted-path tests prove readlink success on real symlinks, but they do not freeze the public error contract for calling readlink on an ordinary file or directory. ffs-core rejects non-symlink inodes and the mounted surface should present Linux EINVAL. Scope: add ext4 mounted-path probes that call readlink on non-symlink paths and assert exact EINVAL without mutating metadata. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes ext4 non-symlink readlink refusal behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:02.498095050Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:43:15.075752349Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:43:15.073875805Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-ebrmet","title":"Saturate MVCC EBR reclaimer counters","description":"`EbrVersionReclaimer` retired/reclaimed version totals still use wrapping atomic `fetch_add`. Long-lived MVCC stores can wrap reclamation telemetry and make pending-version accounting misleading. Route both counters through the existing saturating atomic helper and add numeric-limit coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-fs","created_at":"2026-04-27T10:40:27Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-27T10:40:27Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T10:40:27Z","close_reason":"Routed EBR retired/reclaimed version totals through the saturating atomic helper and added numeric-limit regression coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ece","title":"ffs-block: Cache metrics + instrumentation surface","description":"Goal: expose cache behavior to harness/CLI/TUI so performance work is evidence-based.

Deliverables:
- CacheMetrics struct: hit/miss counts, evictions, current list sizes, p value.
- A cheap snapshot method to read metrics without heavy locks.
- Optional tracing hooks gated behind feature flag.

Acceptance:
- Metrics can be queried from ffs-core and printed by ffs-cli inspect.
- Metrics are used by benchmarks to assert improvements are real (not noise).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:53.854812013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:49:40.556955612Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T20:49:40.556929884Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ece","depends_on_id":"bd-5iz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:16:26.979686708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-edqo","title":"Add load_evidence_records preset+tail coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:42:44.576977883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:46:20.261225902Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T02:46:20.260960850Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-efc2j","title":"[HIGH] ext4 e2compr conformance regressed: inline-data flag remains set on gzip write path","description":"Reproduced from `env AGENT_NAME=cod-fs CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22.

Failure:
- `ext4_e2compr_write_readback_conforms_for_gzip_and_lzo` at `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs:1793`
- assertion: `gzip: e2compr setup must clear inline-data before write-side conformance`
- left: `268435456`
- right: `0`

This looks like a recurrence of the earlier inline-data clearing fix, but the current workspace gate is still red on the gzip path.

Acceptance:
- gzip and lzo write-side conformance both pass
- inline-data flag is cleared before e2compr write-path setup
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` is no longer blocked by this failure slice.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:24:44.522462071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:51:45Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:51:45Z","close_reason":"Masked the historical COMPR/INLINE_DATA alias in ext4 inode interpretation and switched harness e2compr setup to persist a zeroed i_block through OpenFs so gzip/lzo write-side conformance passes without phantom block accounting.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","e2compr","ext4","review"]}
